touches.ai_summary: Cold email sent to Elijah Smith introducing our sales enablement platform and its onboarding automation capabilities. No response received.
Cold call attempt to Elijah Smith. Left voicemail highlighting ROI from onboarding automation. No callback received.
Second cold call attempt to Elijah Smith. No answer, left brief voicemail referencing previous outreach.

Brief 5-minute cold call connected with Elijah Smith. Introduced platform's onboarding automation value prop. Elijah expressed initial interest in learning more given his new Head of RevOps role and focus on scaling processes.

questions_asked.question: What makes your onboarding automation different from what we might already have in place?
questions_asked.answer: Our platform integrates AI-driven workflow automation specifically designed for revenue teams, with native CRM integration and role-based playbooks that adapt as reps progress.

next_steps: Schedule qualification call for next week

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email sent with meeting invite and one-pager on onboarding automation ROI. No response yet.
Second follow-up email with case study from similar eCommerce company. Elijah confirmed meeting time via calendar acceptance.

7-minute qualification call with Elijah. Discussed current onboarding challenges at CoreMetrics. Elijah mentioned he's been in the Head of RevOps role for only 3 months and is evaluating multiple tools including Gong. Confirmed budget authority sits with CRO Dylan Shah.

questions_asked.question: What are your biggest pain points with onboarding new Account Managers today?
questions_asked.answer: Ramp time is too long—takes 4-5 months before AMs are fully productive. We lack consistent process and visibility into where reps struggle.

questions_asked.question: How does your solution compare to Gong, which we're also evaluating?
questions_asked.answer: Gong focuses primarily on conversation intelligence. We provide end-to-end onboarding automation with prescriptive playbooks, milestone tracking, and adaptive coaching—complementary to conversation tools.

next_steps: Share competitive comparison deck
Schedule deeper qualification call to map current process

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email with Gong comparison one-pager and questions for deeper qualification. Elijah responded confirming next call and asking about implementation timeline.

questions_asked.question: If we moved forward, what does implementation typically look like?
questions_asked.answer: Standard implementation is 4-6 weeks with our Customer Success team handling setup, playbook configuration, and training. We include consulting hours to customize workflows to your process.

touches.ai_summary: 18-minute qualification call diving into CoreMetrics' current onboarding process, tech stack, and success metrics. Elijah walked through their 45-person revenue team structure and noted frustration with inconsistent messaging across AMs. Identified key stakeholders: Dylan Shah (CRO, final budget authority), Harper Osei (AM team lead), and Mason Yang (IT, technical evaluation).

questions_asked.question: What metrics would define success for a new onboarding solution?
questions_asked.answer: Reducing ramp time from 4-5 months to under 3 months, increasing first-quarter quota attainment from 45% to 70%, and standardizing our messaging across the team.

questions_asked.question: Who else should be involved in evaluating this beyond yourself?
questions_asked.answer: Dylan Shah, our CRO, will need to sign off. Harper Osei leads our AM team and should see a demo. Mason Yang from IT will need to review integrations and security.

questions_asked.question: What's the pricing structure for 45 seats?
questions_asked.answer: Pricing varies by package—our Automations Plus Consulting tier typically runs $1,800-$2,200 per seat annually depending on implementation scope. I'll put together a custom proposal after discovery.

next_steps: Schedule discovery call to map detailed workflows
Send stakeholder intro email to Dylan, Harper, and Mason

touches.ai_summary: 33-minute discovery call with Elijah covering detailed onboarding workflow mapping. Discussed current tech stack (Salesforce, Outreach, Slack), pain points around content management, and coaching gaps. Elijah shared that Gong demo is scheduled for two weeks out. Emphasized need to move quickly given Q2 planning cycles.

questions_asked.question: Walk me through a new AM's first 90 days today—what does that process look like?
questions_asked.answer: Week 1-2 is general company onboarding and shadowing. Week 3-6 they start taking meetings with support but no ownership. Week 7+ they start owning accounts but often lack confidence. We don't have structured playbooks—it's mostly tribal knowledge from managers.

questions_asked.question: What happens if we can't get you live before Q2 planning wraps up?
questions_asked.answer: That would push us to Q3, which means another quarter of inefficient onboarding. Dylan is pushing hard to have something in place by June.

questions_asked.question: How customizable are the playbooks to our specific sales motion?
questions_asked.answer: Fully customizable. We'd work with your team during implementation to map your methodology, talk tracks, and qualification frameworks into the playbooks. The consulting hours in the package cover exactly that.

questions_asked.question: What kind of reporting does your platform provide to leadership?
questions_asked.answer: We provide dashboards showing onboarding milestone completion, time-to-productivity by rep, skill gap analysis, and manager coaching activity—all exportable and integrated with your CRM reporting.

objections: We're also looking at Gong and want to make sure we're not duplicating functionality.
next_steps: Send detailed discovery recap and ROI calculator
Prepare demo tailored to CoreMetrics workflows
Loop in Dylan Shah for initial intro call

touches.ai_summary: 22-minute follow-up call with Elijah to review discovery notes and confirm demo agenda. Discussed differentiators vs. Gong in detail. Elijah confirmed Dylan Shah is available for a brief intro call next week. Mentioned budget is allocated but needs to show clear ROI given other competing priorities.

questions_asked.question: Can you integrate with our existing Outreach sequences?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we have native Outreach integration that syncs activity data and can trigger onboarding milestones based on sequence completion.

questions_asked.question: What's Dylan's biggest concern or priority when it comes to onboarding?
questions_asked.answer: Speed and consistency. He wants new hires productive faster and wants everyone selling the same way—our messaging is all over the place right now.

next_steps: Schedule demo for next week
Set up 15-minute intro call with Dylan Shah
Send Gong comparison deep-dive doc

touches.ai_summary: 15-minute call with Elijah to finalize demo attendees and agenda. Confirmed Harper Osei and Mason Yang will attend. Elijah mentioned some internal budget discussions happening but seemed confident in moving forward. Briefly touched on timeline expectations.

questions_asked.question: Should we include anyone from the AM team in the demo beyond Harper?
questions_asked.answer: Let's keep it tight for now—Harper can represent the team and cascade feedback. If we move forward we can do a broader enablement session.

next_steps: Confirm demo logistics
Send pre-demo questionnaire to Harper and Mason

touches.ai_summary: 45-minute demo with Elijah, Harper Osei, and Mason Yang. Walked through onboarding playbook creation, milestone tracking, AI-driven coaching recommendations, and manager dashboards. Harper was highly engaged and asked detailed questions about rep experience. Mason focused on security, SSO, and Salesforce integration. Overall positive reception with strong interest in customization capabilities.

questions_asked.question: How do reps actually interact with this day-to-day? Is it another tool they have to log into?
questions_asked.answer: It's embedded directly in Salesforce and Slack where your reps already work. They get nudges and access playbooks in context—no separate login required for daily use.

questions_asked.question: What does the security review process look like? Do you have SOC 2?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we're SOC 2 Type II certified. We can provide our security documentation and schedule a technical deep-dive with your IT team if needed.

questions_asked.question: Can we tailor the coaching prompts to our specific value prop and competitor landscape?
questions_asked.answer: Absolutely. During implementation we build custom coaching libraries based on your talk tracks, objection handling, and competitive positioning—including Gong if that's a common comparison your reps face.

objections: We need to make sure this doesn't create extra admin burden for managers who are already stretched thin.
next_steps: Send demo recording and feature summary
Schedule technical deep-dive with Mason
Prepare custom proposal with ROI projection

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email with demo recap, recording, and ROI calculator showing potential $450K annual impact from faster ramp. Harper responded enthusiastically. Mason requested security docs and technical deep-dive call.

35-minute exec meeting with Dylan Shah and Elijah Smith. Presented business case and ROI projections. Dylan focused on competitive landscape, asking pointed questions about Gong differentiation and why not just use conversation intelligence. Discussed budget and noted that $90K+ would require additional approvals from Elena Ito and Rachel Hall in IT/procurement. Dylan expressed interest but wanted to see pricing options.

questions_asked.question: Why shouldn't we just go with Gong and use their onboarding modules?
questions_asked.answer: Gong excels at conversation intelligence and deal insights but doesn't provide structured onboarding automation, prescriptive playbooks, or milestone-based enablement. Many customers use both—they're complementary solutions.

questions_asked.question: What's the pricing flexibility if we wanted to start with a smaller pilot group?
questions_asked.answer: We can structure a phased approach, but our licensing model works best at 30+ seats to justify the implementation investment. I can show options in the proposal.

questions_asked.question: What's your timeline for making a decision?
questions_asked.answer: We need something in place by early June for Q2 planning. I'd like to see a proposal within the week and make a call by end of month.

objections: The price point is higher than I expected—we need to make sure the ROI is ironclad.
next_steps: Send formal proposal with tiered pricing options
Include competitive analysis vs. Gong
Loop in Elena Ito and Rachel Hall for procurement review

touches.ai_summary: 21-minute follow-up demo with Elijah focusing on advanced reporting and manager coaching workflows. Elijah mentioned Dylan is warming up but price-sensitive. Discussed potential to reduce scope slightly to hit budget targets.

questions_asked.question: If we reduced consulting hours, could we bring the price down?
questions_asked.answer: We could trim consulting from 40 to 20 hours, which would reduce ACV by about $8-10K, but implementation would be more DIY on your side.

next_steps: Update proposal with reduced consulting option
Schedule proposal review call with Dylan and Elijah

touches.ai_summary: 36-minute exec meeting with Dylan Shah and Elijah to review formal proposal. Presented three pricing tiers: $99K (full consulting), $88K (reduced consulting), $78K (self-serve implementation). Dylan pushed back on seat count, suggesting 40 seats instead of 45. Discussed contract terms and payment structure. Dylan wants to involve Elena Ito and Rachel Hall before final decision.

questions_asked.question: What happens if we only need 40 seats instead of 45?
questions_asked.answer: We can adjust to 40 seats. At the reduced consulting tier, that would bring ACV to approximately $82K.

questions_asked.question: Can we do quarterly payments instead of annual upfront?
questions_asked.answer: We can structure quarterly payments with a small finance fee, or offer a 5% discount for annual prepay.

questions_asked.question: What's the next step to move this forward?
questions_asked.answer: I need to run this by Elena and Rachel from our side, then we can finalize terms and get contracts moving.

objections: We're still comparing this to the Gong proposal which came in lower, even though it's not apples-to-apples.
next_steps: Send updated proposal for 45 seats at $88.2K (reduced consulting)
Schedule review call with Elena Ito and Rachel Hall
Provide Gong comparison one-pager for Dylan to share internally

touches.ai_summary: Email to Elijah with revised proposal at $88.2K for 45 seats, automations plus consulting package. Included payment term options and implementation timeline. Elijah confirmed he's sharing with Dylan, Elena, and Rachel.

19-minute negotiation call with Elijah Smith (rescheduled from previous week). Discussed final pricing and contract terms. Elijah mentioned internal pressure to reduce costs but acknowledged the value. Confirmed 45 seats at $88.2K is the target. Mentioned Elena and Rachel need to review security and vendor agreements. Dylan wants final decision by end of week.

questions_asked.question: Is there any flexibility on the $88.2K number if we commit to a multi-year deal?
questions_asked.answer: For a two-year commitment we could lock pricing at $88.2K for year one and $85K for year two with expanded seats. I'd need to confirm with leadership.

objections: Procurement is flagging the quarterly payment finance fee as an issue—can we waive that?
next_steps: Check on multi-year pricing option
Waive quarterly payment fee to expedite close
Send revised agreement to Elena and Rachel

touches.ai_summary: 36-minute exec meeting with Dylan Shah, Elijah, and rep to finalize terms. Dylan confirmed budget approval at $88.2K pending legal and security review. Discussed implementation timeline and success metrics. Dylan emphasized importance of onboarding completion by June 15 for Q2 readiness. Positive tone but clear expectations on delivery.

questions_asked.question: What happens if implementation runs over the 6-week timeline?
questions_asked.answer: We commit to the June 15 go-live. If delays occur on our side, we'll add extra consulting hours at no cost to get you across the finish line.

questions_asked.question: Are there any remaining internal hurdles we should be aware of?
questions_asked.answer: Just legal and security sign-off from Elena's team. Elijah will drive that. Assuming no surprises, we're good to go.

next_steps: Send final contract with agreed terms
Coordinate legal review with Elena Ito
Schedule kickoff call for week of contract signature

touches.ai_summary: 35-minute final exec meeting with Dylan Shah and Elijah to review executed contracts and confirm kickoff. Dylan signed off on final terms: 45 seats, $88.2K ACV, automations plus consulting package, quarterly payments (fee waived). Discussed success criteria and quarterly business reviews. Dylan expressed confidence in partnership and emphasized urgency on June 15 deadline. Contracts sent to Elena Ito and Rachel Hall for countersignature.

questions_asked.question: Who will be our main point of contact during implementation?
questions_asked.answer: You'll be assigned a dedicated Customer Success Manager who will lead implementation and ongoing support. I'll introduce you on the kickoff call.

next_steps: Obtain final countersignatures from Elena and Rachel
Schedule implementation kickoff for April 28
Assign CS Manager and send welcome packet
